@maheshj01 
Thank you very much for checking. I also believe this issue is similar to #124483. However, #124483 only mentions iOS, and it states that the cursor moves to a completely different location, so I created a new issue.

@maheshj01 

This problem is not unique to textfield. It's a common problem with SelectableText.rich() or many other text selection widgets on the web and it has been going on for chrome based browsers for almost years. The selection cursor jumps to places other than the selected area. However, when the selection is made and the mouse is released, we see that the selected area is actually selected. Also, once you try to make a selection and release the mouse, strangely the unrelated textfields above start to hover as if the mouse is over them. Once you hover and click on them, this problem is fixed until the next text selection. I'm leaving some example videos below.

Also these issues seem to be similar:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/162231
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/162698

Native SelectableText.rich() example:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85a2f4cc-37a4-4ef7-8dbd-daca0012d308

Some custom package re_editor usage example:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79605503-c864-4815-bb50-6a9c4eef9a51

Seems to happen on chrome and chrome based browsers like Arc. Safari seems to be fine. I don't know about other browsers, I haven't tried them.

Current sdk and flutter version I'm working on:
sdk: ">=3.6.1 <4.0.0"
flutter: ">=3.27.3 <4.0.0"

Can be examined at: [Flutter Advanced Boilerplate Generator](https://generator.flutterboilerplate.dev/)

Hi @mycalls, I tried to reproduce this behavior on the current `master`, and the cursor moves to the correct place (see the recording).
Could you please try to reproduce the bug on your side on the `master` channel?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/762e3d1f-af03-4e24-9cbb-51767866b4a9

Hi @mycalls, I tried again and reproduced the bug. There are jumps on the Android 😔
You can verify this in the following web app: https://ios-web-cursor-positioning.web.app
Hi @ksokolovskyi, thank you so much for not only confirming the bug but also taking the time to create a test app — I truly appreciate it.

I am also experiencing this issue. What is frustrating to me is on my local builds I do not have the issue, but once I deploy my application the issue arises. I am not sure if that is any clue to help those who are trying to solve this bug, but it is something I have been really banging my head on my keyboard over since I can't even test anymore since I don't know if what runs locally will be the same as what ends up being deployed, so I have had to deploy to test.

issues.title: [android] Textfield text selection handlers get covered by keyboard
comments:
Reproducible using the code sample provided above. A reduced sample has been provided below. I checked with the `Telegram` and `Whatsapp` native android apps and the selection handlers do appear above the keyboard but the opposite occurs in flutter. On iOS, I attempted to reproduce this with the notes app and it looks like the selection handlers are drawn below toolbars and possibly the keyboard (I did test with a swiftui native app, and the handler was below the keyboard). Labeling this as an android-specific issue.
screenshots | Telegram | Flutter app | iOS Notes | | -- | -- | -- | | | | |
code samples ### Flutter code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { MyApp({super.key}); final _messageController = TextEditingController(); final _focusNode = FocusNode(); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( title: 'Flutter Demo', theme: ThemeData( primarySwatch: Colors.blue, ), home: Scaffold( appBar: AppBar( title: Text('Text Selection Handlers Issue'), ), body: Column( crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.end, children: [ TextFormField( controller: _messageController, minLines: 1, maxLines: 12, keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline, textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.sentences, focusNode: _focusNode, onTap: () {}, decoration: const InputDecoration( border: InputBorder.none, isDense: true, hintText: "Message", ), ), ], ), ), ); } } ``` ### SWIFT UI Sample for confirming iOS behavior ```swift import SwiftUI struct ContentView: View { @State private var textValue = "" var body: some View { VStack(alignment: .trailing) { Spacer() TextField("Text", text: $textValue) } .padding() } } struct ContentView_Previews: PreviewProvider { static var previews: some View { ContentView() } } ```

@danagbemava-nc ios note app is a scrollable view, so user can at least moves the text away from keyboard to see the handlers.

With iMessage handlers appear above the keyboard. 

[edited] Handlers shape seems to also help on iOS. I mean that rendering textfield inside a small messaging bar is enough to keep them away from keyboard.

![8556B66C-DF20-4AB5-9BFB-E972521FCC0F](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/53510751/218427788-66a40e96-7c75-4989-b3fd-71f24b57ab80.png)

This might not be practical to fix.  It's not possible for the Flutter view to draw on top of the keyboard right now.  @reidbaker Do you know if it would even be possible/practical to rearrange these views so that it's possible to draw a handle on top of the keyboard?

In the meantime, the solution might be to just make sure that your field is far enough away from the edge of the screen that this won't happen.  I know that's not a nice solution...

There is not a straightforward way to to draw over the keyboard that I know of. Some clever stackoverflow engineers have found ways to use custom styled dialogs or popups. https://stackoverflow.com/questions/16060579/how-to-draw-view-on-top-of-soft-keyboard-like-whatsapp 

That said with the way flutter is built to use those methods I think we would have the added complexity of layering in a native android view instead of relying on the flutter layer to do compositing and rendering. 

My suggestion is to have this bug focus on positioning or sizing of the handles and not drawing over the keyboard.
As a practical matter I think drawing on top of the keyboard will create other issues.

Thank you @reidbaker!

I wonder if there's anything Flutter should do to avoid the handle going outside of the view.  @delfme Could you check on native Android if it's possible for the handle to go past the bottom of the screen (when the keyboard is closed)?  If it does anything to avoid it, like flipping the handle above the text, then maybe Flutter could do the same thing in the case of overlapping the keyboard.
@justinmc Im not into android but at first glance it looks like it doesnt.
@reidbaker unfortunately if workaround is to move textfield far from keyboard, result will be ugly in that specific use case.

Trying this on a Kotlin app really quickly, I think Android allows the handles to hang off the screen:

I don't see anything that Flutter can do right now to alleviate this problem unfortunately.

@darshankawar I am trying to build a strongly typed FormField for Locations. 
In that FF I decided to use a TextFormField thereby building a castellated Tree of FormFields.

Once the Form is being saved I would expect that all changes would bubble up to the root and thereby modifying the `Map state` - which is only for the minimal example.

While debugging the widget I saw that all onSaves were called but the change of `FormField<Location>` with the didChange wasn't pulled up to the root
Thanks for the update. Keeping the issue open and labeling for team's input on actual vs expected behavior.

Issue is assigned to multiple teams (design, framework). Please ensure the issue has only one `team-*` label at a time. Use `fyi-*` labels to have another team look at the issue without reassigning it.

I think you're expecting the `onSaved` callbacks of your fields to be called in something like a depth-first traversal order? In the [code](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7e05bc4b302a48ca27fae68ccdd6a4cdef25ce68/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/form.dart#L270) you can see that it's just iterating the Set of fields after they've been registered.

Some things you could try:

  * Make use of Form.onChanged, which is called after all of its fields have been saved.
  * Do all of the onSaved Location saving logic in your parent FormField's onSaved. Do individual saving logic for the nested TextFormFields in onFieldSubmitted.

I see why you would expect the onSaved of the parent to be called first, but that's not how this is architected. I'm not sure what change would be made to correct this, likely some bigger refactor.

issues.title: [macOS] TextField left arrow key while composing deletes text inside composing range
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Use Japanese as an input source
3. **(Don't skip this step)** Type anything and confirm out of its composing state. Make sure the typed letters in this step are not in the composing range. The error doesn't seem to occur if the entire text editing value of the TextField is within the composing range. The idea is to make sure there is at least one letter preceding the error causing composing region. 
4. Type any letter after step 3, and hit space key to bring up a composing candidate list
5. Press the left arrow key, at least 2 times for a single character or more for longer character sequence.
6. Then you should see the text in the composing range disappears. 

### Other notes & concerns
1. I'm not sure if this is an intended behavior. At least other text editors don't behave like this. 
2. Pressing any arrow key while composing makes macOS ring an error sound. It's the sound you often hear when you perform unintended operations. This is actually a much bigger concern. What's going on? Hope it doesn't lead to another issue in the future...

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
The text inside composing region doesn't disappear with left arrow keys, nor does pressing any arrow key produces any error sound. 

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
This is me pressing left arrow on my keyboard when text inside composing range disappears.

Thanks for the report @K-Maron 
Is the behavior only occuring on desktop platform, or on mobile too (ex: ios simulator) and on web ?

I used your code sample and ran it on macOS desktop platform using latest master version of the framework.
I first selected Japanese Kana language and entered `test` word and followed your steps to replicate. Tried left arrow key which didn't close the composing range disappear.
I then tried with `abc`, hit space and then left arrow key with which I probably saw the composing range disappear.

I am not completely sure if this is the correct interpretation or representation of the behavior you reported, but I observed as shown in below video:

> * **(Don't skip this step)** Type anything and confirm out of its composing state. Make sure the typed letters in this step are not in the composing range. The error doesn't seem to occur if the entire text editing value of the TextField is within the composing range. The idea is to make sure there is at least one letter preceding the error causing composing region.
> * Type any letter after step 3, and hit space key to bring up a composing candidate list

If above is not completely correct that represents your issue, please provide sample text that we can enter as-is (using English keyboard) so that we can use it to verify this properly.

Also, I see that you are on `3.3.10`, so I also suggest you to run your scenario on latest master or stable to check if the behavior still persists on them or not.

> (Don't skip this step) Type anything and confirm out of its composing state. Make sure the typed letters in this step are not in the composing range. The error doesn't seem to occur if the entire text editing value of the TextField is within the composing range. The idea is to make sure there is at least one letter preceding the error causing composing region.

From the video you recorded, I can see that you are actually skipping this step above. Composing range should not take up the whole text of the TextField in order for this issue to be reproduced. There has to be other text preceding the error causing composing range. 

To illustrate my point further, for example, type keyboard "A" to produce "あ" and hit enter to exit composing mode for this character. Then type keyboard "U" to produce "う" and this time press left arrow key at least twice or more while in composing and see if the error happens. As I said the preceding "あ" here is needed to be out of the error causing composing range "う". 

To further clarify my point above, please check this [flutter api doc](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/TextEditingController/buildTextSpan.html) to find an implementation of buildTextSpan method in TextEditingController class.
```dart
TextSpan(text: value.composing.textBefore(value.text)),
TextSpan(
  style: composingStyle,
  text: value.composing.textInside(value.text),
),
TextSpan(text: value.composing.textAfter(value.text)),
```

What I mean by saying preceding letters need to exist for this error to reproduce is that value.composing.textBefore(value.text) should not be empty! So in the example provided above, value.composing.textBefore(value.text) = "あ" and value.composing.textInside(value.text) = "う". In your video, you have value.composing.textBefore(value.text) = "" as empty string. By this, you won't be able to reproduce the error.  Hope I'm clear here.

Regarding flutter version upgrade, I checked the release note of 3.7.0 and change logs before posing this issue. It doesn't seem like anything related to this issue is addressed. I'll see if I can find time to go through the upgrade, but please check on your side as well if you can reproduce the error based on the further explanation above. Steps to reproduce provided initially is still correct it seems to me at least... 

Lastly, I'm also using US keyboard ;)

issues.title: WebViewWidget steals keyboard focus from a TextField on touch
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Have a screen with a `WebViewWidget` (non-empty) and a `TextField`.
2. Focus the text field and type something. It works.
3. Tap the web view. The text field still has the focus, but the tapping the keys does nothing, and also autocomplete is gone.

I tried wrapping the web view in `ExcludeFocus` and `TextFieldTapRegion`, and adding an `onTapOutside` listener to the text field. Nothing changed this behaviour.

I am testing on Android 14 phone, Sony Xperia 10 IV.

### Expected results

I expect tapping the non-interactive web view to have no effect on the textfield.

### Actual results

When tapping on the web view, the autocomplete bar is hidden, but the keyboard stays visible, and there's a blinking cursor inside the text field. Tapping any of the keyboard keys does not work.

```dart
}

    );
  }

  @override

          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

Thanks for the report! 

I also checked this on iOS app, when I tap on WebView, the TextField will lose focus and keyboard is dismissed, I think this should be the correct behavior. 

Marking this issue on Android platform. This may be somewhat related to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/130652.

Package version: `webview_flutter_android: ^4.0.0`
Flutter versions: `Channel stable, 3.24.3` and `Channel master, 3.27.0-1.0.pre.35`

To clarify my intent, I want the WebView to serve as a non-tappable reference, akin to rich text in a ListView. That is, to TextField to not lose focus here. I tried all the means to keep the focus, but it seems, WebView doesn't play by rules wrt focus.

I have a similar problem on Web (only reproducible in Firefox, Chrome doesn't do it):
When you have a TextField in front of/wrapped in an HTML Widget (in my case it is a PointerInterceptor), the WebView widget steals the keyboard focus away from the TextField whenever it is tapped, even when tapping the TextField itself, which is on top of it. That means that the text field essentially becomes unusable on Firefox Web.

issues.title: Text field loses focus when changing Gboard language on Android
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

On a Pixel 8 with Gboard:
1. Run the app with the provided sample code
2. Change the keyboard input language

### Expected results

The text field stays focused and the keyboard stays visible, so I can keep typing.

This is the behavior of all other apps on my phone.

The text field loses focus and the keyboard disappears.

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

    );
  }

      appBar: AppBar(
```

I can reproduce this on my physical Pixel 6, though only when I cause the "Change keyboard" dialog to appear and make a selection, not when I just tap the globe 🌐 key to immediately switch languages.

I've confirmed that this is not coming from the framework. The framework does not send [TextInput.hide](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/d9cf066191dd0188d517ce92220d71a1887a2988/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L2315) when I reproduce the bug.

I see the engine calls hideSoftInputFromWindow in two places, maybe one of those are causing it to close: https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/8eba63f29b8b26931496807cb4fc2fed8e4e3258/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/editing/TextInputPlugin.java#L371, https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/8eba63f29b8b26931496807cb4fc2fed8e4e3258/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/editing/TextInputPlugin.java#L401.

And here's an old issue that sounds vaguely similar: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/34169.

Maybe someone with more Android expertise would have a hunch @reidbaker ?

*Edit*: Oh and I see this in my console when I reproduce it:

```
I/ImeTracker(15092): com.example.sandbox:eb0c645b: onRequestHide at ORIGIN_CLIENT_HIDE_SOFT_INPUT reason HIDE_SOFT_INPUT_BY_INSETS_API
```
This does not happen if the app is run as a web app. No idea if that's relevant.

I can reproduce this issues using various emulators on Android 34, 33, 32 and 31, but not on 30. Each time using the tip of the tree (which is `Channel master, 3.23.0-13.0.pre.197` at this moment).
I also tried after commenting the calls to `hideSoftInputFromWindow` on the engine.

Because neither the framework nor the engine send a direct call to close the keyboard, it might be related to non-obvious APIs used by the Flutter engine or to the default configuration (manifest file having or missing some parameters).
During my investigation, I commented out a large chunk of the engine code, but was still able to reproduce. I might try to pursue the investigation but it is very tricky.
The fact that it does not repro on Android 30 is interesting.
Interesting, I thought for sure it would be one of the calls to hideSoftInputFromWindow. Not sure why else Android might be closing the keyboard. Thanks for doing that investigation @bleroux.
I just tested, and it looks like this is now working for me on my Pixel 7! It may have something to do with the recent Android 15 upgrade, but I'm not sure. My Flutter version is 3.24.1 (stable).

Thanks @Anderillo!
I can confirm that the issue does not reproduce on my Pixel 7 with Android 15.

I checked another time and unfortunately this is still reproducible. As pointed out in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/148117#issuecomment-2121013974, it happens only when causing the "Change keyboard" dialog to appear and make a selection, not when tapping the globe 🌐 key to immediately switch languages.

Just to follow up on this: I have since switched away from Gboard on my Pixel 8 because developing and dogfooding [my own Flutter app](https://chill-chinese.com/chinese-dictionary/) was too much of a pain.

issues.title: FocusNode.skipTraversal is set unexpectedly through Focus widget
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Focus on the text field. Click + button twice. **Press tab to change focus.**

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Text field is traversable.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Text field is not traversable.

**Root cause:**

I believe the issue is here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/18a827f3933c19f51862dde3fa472197683249d6/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/focus_scope.dart#L607-L612

Should be:

```
        if (widget._skipTraversal != focusNode.skipTraversal) {
          focusNode.skipTraversal = widget._skipTraversal;
        }
        if (widget._canRequestFocus != focusNode.canRequestFocus) {
          focusNode.canRequestFocus = widget._canRequestFocus!;
        }
```

Could someone confirm if my finding is correct?

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>




<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(home: MyHomePage());
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key});

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  bool descendantsAreTraversable = true;

  void _changeDescendantsAreTraversable() {
    setState(() {
      descendantsAreTraversable = !descendantsAreTraversable;
    });
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: Focus(
        descendantsAreTraversable: descendantsAreTraversable,
        child: Center(
          child: Column(
            mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
            children: <Widget>[
              TextButton(onPressed: () {}, child: const Text('A')),

              Checkbox(value: false, onChanged: (value) {}),
              Checkbox(value: false, onChanged: (value) {}),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
      floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(
        onPressed: _changeDescendantsAreTraversable,
        child: const Icon(Icons.add),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: ScrollViews need to be direct children of Scaffold for TextFields to scroll into focus
comments:
I believe you're referring to the keyboard pushing up your list if you select something at the bottom? 

If so, then that's a `Scaffold` property called `resizeToAvoidBottomPadding` which is set to true by default. So if you set `resizeToAvoidBottomPadding: false` within your main scaffold, you'll get the same behavior as the new screen where the keyboard covers your selected item.

@OsaXma I'd like to make sure when you tap into a `TextField` it scrolls into focus. I updated my issue to more clearly reflect that. I would think that you would only need a parent `Scaffold` in the hierarchy in order for screens that are pushed on to scroll their `TextField`s into focus. It seems unwieldy to have to wrap every screen in a new `Scaffold` if I just want the `TextField`s to scroll into focus.

issues.title: [Web]: Pointer acts incorrectly when typing at TextField wrapped in Stack
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create project with represented code sample. 
2. Run at Flutter Web (I use Chrome)
3. Click to TextField, write something
4. Move pointer to a Button 1

Expected result: Pointer is at click state
Actual result: Pointer is at text editing state

Note: Tried to wrap TextField with MouseRegion - didn't help
Note 2: Works properly if to run as desktop app on  macOs

How it works on web:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17158960/202515244-ff808b02-2f46-4372-b563-cf7b1669c37d.mov

<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Pointer at stack',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
```

@Renzo-Olivares was thinking this might have to do with the [deleteBackwards](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/42df55a9e63ed3a8f0c70239c046e9242aad2660/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm#L2054-L2069) code in the iOS embedder.

The iOS text input plugin is receiving a call to `setSelectedTextRange` before the call to `deleteBackward`.

The `setSelectedTextRange` call sends an `updateEditingState` message to the framework with a selection range containing the character that will be deleted.  The state's `selection.baseOffset` will be the offset of that character (not the cursor position before the delete).

That editing state is then provided as the `oldValue` when the framework invokes `TextInputFormatter.formatEditUpdate`.

issues.title: Better textFormField label placement customization when focused and when value input- vertical floatingLabelAlignment feature.
comments: Hello @SexyBeast007. Thank you for filing this request. This issue goes against the Material guidelines for text fields you can find [here](https://material.io/components/text-fields). Since Flutter follows Material guidelines, this does not seem like a valid request to me. Therefore, I am closing this issue. If you disagree, please comment and we can reopen it. Thank you.

@exaby73 Can you please tell me specifically what this proposal collides with in Material guidelines?

I searched and couldn't find anything specific. As a matter of fact, my design is very similar to underline text fields in that the label floats up just above the input content. Surely a border around it all doesn't hinder anything that I could find.

If you see the section on [label text](https://material.io/components/text-fields#anatomy), you see 2 types of labels used, one with the filled text field which is similar to yours and one with the outline text field which cuts the top border of the text field.

I am in no way an expert here, so I'll label this issue for further insights from the team. Thank you.
Just curious how long it might take to have something like this implemented if is accepted?

There any update on this issue?

I am in a similar boat where design calls for the label to not overlap the border.

I have a very similar issue to, but I've solved almost all of it, except one part that is hard coded deep in the flutter framework (which I've put in a pull request to parameterize). 

I'll share what I did here, but take a look at my issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/130030.

So I broke the problem down into 3 different problems.
1. background color and shape, and placement
2. border
3. label placement

My situation is easier because I don't have a (responsive) border to deal with. But my background is curved. So I made the TextField transparent and borderless. Then I wrap it in a `Stack` with a `Container` behind that has the correct shape and placement of that shape. Using the `contentPadding` in the `TextField` I can control where the text shows up, how far away the error text is, etc, I place it below the shape.

The only thing I don't have control over here distance between the label and the text content. that's why I issued a pull request to parameterize that part. once that's accepted I'll be able to specify `floatingLabelGap: 0.0` in the `InputDecoration` and the label will show where it is in our design.

As you can see this is a piecemeal solution, which is never ideal, but sufficient. This isn't a whole perfect solution, but it solves the issue with minimal addition to (and no alteration of) the flutter framework.

```
Stack(children: [
  Column(children: [
    SizedBox(height: 4),
    Container(
      height: 64,
      decoration: ShapeDecoration(
        color: Color(0xFFE8EAF6),
        shape: RoundedRectangleBorder(borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(28 * 100)),
      ),
    ),
  ]),
  TextField(
    decoration: InputDecoration(
      border: InputBorder.none,
      alignLabelWithHint: true,
      contentPadding: EdgeInsets.only(left: 20, top: 14, right: 14, bottom: 20),

      /*** might not be necessary to specify, but might be helpful to see: ***/
      filled: false,
      fillColor: Colors.transparent,
      focusColor: Colors.transparent,
      focusedErrorBorder: InputBorder.none,
      errorBorder: InputBorder.none,
      focusedBorder: InputBorder.none,
      enabledBorder: InputBorder.none,
      disabledBorder: InputBorder.none,
      /*** end of might not be necessary to specify ***/

      ...
    ),
    ...
]);
```
Does @lastmeta's PR (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/130039) solve this issue for anyone experiencing this?
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

This issue is assigned to @lastmeta but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

> This issue is assigned to @lastmeta but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

For anyone who is curious, the reason this issue hasn't had activity is I'm too tired to fight for it. It's an (above all else) simple, but also easy, effective, and minimal solution. But person after person pushed back on it. Until finally someone suggested they know of a better (unarticulated and still yet unmaterialized) way to handle the situation altogether. Ok then.

Congrats, the bureaucracy won, my many hours of effort to implement a tiny improvement to the code base, amounting to essentially *one line* of code was simply no match for the it!

For those who might encounter a similar issue, here is my workaround. I created my implementation of `InputBorder` (similar to `OutlineInputBorder` or `UnderlineInputBorder`). The key difference is that it positions the label inside the border, eliminating any gap on the border for the label. Here's the [gist](https://gist.github.com/Vanchel/d473ef4204ab54b6203020bc4f43c994).

I hope this solution can be convenient as it doesn't introduce any additional wrappers and can be used quite universally. For example, you can use this `InputBorder` implementation directly in your theme.

There are relatively few differences from the `OutlineInputBorder` implementation to be fair, and most of the code is simply copied. The main change is that the `isOutline` getter returns false (similar to `UnderlineInputBorder`), as this value determines where the label will be positioned. Keep in mind that this value is taken into account for some other minor parameters, so you would possibly want to check how `InputDecorator` handles it to ensure that my solution suits your needs. For example, you may want to specify custom `contentPadding` value. I also modified the logic for drawing the border on the canvas to avoid adding any gap for the label, but there shouldn't be any unexpected issues here, as far as I understand.

You can also use the floating label on a self-built fieldset: ```dart class PlatformFieldSet extends StatelessWidget { const PlatformFieldSet({ super.key, required this.labelText, required this.children, }); final String labelText; final List children; @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { final theme = Theme.of(context); final onMaterial = isMaterial(context); return Padding( padding: const EdgeInsetsDirectional.fromSTEB(10.0, 22.0, 10.0, 20.0), child: InputDecorator( decoration: InputDecoration( labelText: this.labelText, filled: !onMaterial, fillColor: !onMaterial ? theme.brightness == Brightness.light ? CupertinoColors.systemBackground.resolveFrom(context) : CupertinoColors.systemGrey5.resolveFrom(context) : Colors.transparent, isDense: !onMaterial, contentPadding: !onMaterial ? const EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(10.0, 32.0, -5.0, 0.0) : null, floatingLabelStyle: onMaterial ? theme.inputDecorationTheme.floatingLabelStyle : theme.textTheme.titleMedium!.apply( color: theme .inputDecorationTheme.floatingLabelStyle?.color), enabledBorder: onMaterial ? OutlineInputBorder( borderSide: BorderSide( color: theme.colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest)) : OutlineInputBorder( borderSide: BorderSide.none, borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular( 10.0)) // from CupertinoListSection._kDefaultInsetGroupedBorderRadius ), child: Column( mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: this.children))); } } ``` I like it so, but now I wish I could tell it to move the floating text within the container, not onto the border, when on iOS. Edit a contact in iOS Contacts and you know what I mean. So I wish to advocate not just for adjusting the placement on the border, but also if its outside or inside the container.

This issue is assigned to @lastmeta but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

issues.title: iOS: Verification code autofill duplicates when `CFBundleLocalizations` is defined as `zh_CN` in  `Info.plist`
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Flutter app with a verification code input field.
The minimal reproducible sample project is attached — you can view repo directly [here](https://github.com/HuangRunHua/flutter_duplicate_sms_code_demo)
 or download it [sms_demo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22861450/sms_demo.zip).

2. In `ios/Runner/Info.plist`, add the following configuration:
```xml
<key>CFBundleLocalizations</key>
<array>
    <string>zh_CN</string>
</array>
```

3. Build and run the app on a physical iOS device.

4. Send an SMS or email verification code to the device.

5. Tap the autofill suggestion bar above the keyboard (iOS autofill feature).


> Detail video: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8f008e2d-ecc0-4ddb-8529-4197ac21b304



### Actual results

- The verification code autofills twice, or duplicates in the text field. (For example, a code "123456" becomes "123456123456".)
- Removing the <string>zh_CN</string> section from `Info.plist` immediately fixes the issue.

### Logs

```console

<!-- Paste your logs here -->

```

</details>

comments:
@ctkqiang Thanks for your detailed reply!

I’ll try adding the `textContentType: TextContentType.oneTimeCode` and related configurations later to see if that helps.

Currently, the minimal reproducible project already keeps only the necessary languages in `CFBundleLocalizations`. If I completely remove the `CFBundleLocalizations` field from the `Info.plist`, the problem disappears and the code input works as expected.

Do you have any idea why this happens? I’m curious about the root cause — why would having `CFBundleLocalizations` defined cause iOS to autofill the one-time code twice?

Thanks again for your help!

@HuangRunHua Thanks for the report. I tried the scenario using iOS device with latest stable version (3.35.6) but was unable to see the duplicated verification code. See below: I see that you are on Xcode 26 which I am not. Wondering if it is related to the version or not. Is there a way for you to check if this occurs with your previous xcode version as well (ie, Xcode < 26) ? Also, does the device need to be in non-English locale to replicate this ? Currently my device is in Eng locale. Also, can you narrow down the BundleLocalizations values to minimum only with which the issue occurs still ?

@darshankawar Thanks for following up! I’ve just tested again using **Xcode 16.4** (previous stable version), and the issue **still occurs**.
To help you better reproduce it, I’ve now minimized the `CFBundleLocalizations` array to only **`zh_CN`**, and pushed this change to the repo.
You can find the updated minimal reproducible project [here](https://github.com/HuangRunHua/flutter_duplicate_sms_code_demo)

I also made a few additional tests and found something interesting:
* When I switch the **iOS system language to English** (without changing the region or Xcode), the code autofill works **normally** — it fills only once.
* When I switch the **language to Simplified Chinese**, the issue **reappears**, regardless of the selected region.
* If I **remove `zh_CN`** from `CFBundleLocalizations`, the issue **disappears completely**, even when other localization entries (like `en`, `ja`, `ko`, etc.) are still present.

So it seems the root cause is specifically related to the **presence of `zh_CN` in `CFBundleLocalizations`** or the **system language being Simplified Chinese**, rather than the region or Xcode version.

Hope this helps narrow it down further!

Thanks for the update @HuangRunHua I tried by changing system language of my iPhone to Simplified Chinese but wasn't able to still replicate the reported behavior. I tried with latest stable and master versions. I also tried with sms and email verification codes but they don't duplicate in the textfield. Are you running on iOS 26 version on your physical device ?

@darshankawar Thanks for checking! I’ve tested on two devices with Flutter 3.35.4 and 3.35.6 channel stable:

- iOS 18.6.2 (iPhone XR)
- iOS 26.0.1 (iPhone 12 Pro Max)

The issue still occurs on both. You can try using a Chinese verification message like this:
```
【XXXX】验证码703497。如非本人操作，请忽略本短信。
```

The duplication happens when the system autofills this kind of Chinese SMS verification code.

> You can try using a Chinese verification message like this:
> 
> ```
> 【XXXX】验证码703497。如非本人操作，请忽略本短信。
> ```
> 
> The duplication happens when the system autofills this kind of Chinese SMS verification code.

I am unsure how to do that in non-English language as I believe it'll need sender's system to be configured for the language.
Based on the report though and behavior, I'll keep the issue open and label for team's tracking.

@darshankawar Thanks for your reply! I understand that testing with a non-English language would require the sender’s system to be configured accordingly. 

However, it is possible to test this simply by using another device to compose and send the SMS to the test device — no special SMS configuration is needed.

Glad to know the issue is being tracked — please let me know if any additional info or testing from my side could help.

Thanks again for your support!

> it is possible to test this simply by using another device to compose and send the SMS to the test device — no special SMS configuration is needed. Yes, I tried with this approach as well, but still couldn't replicate.

@darshankawar I tried a few more times on an **iPhone XR** with iOS 18.6.2 and found some clues that might help with your investigation:

1. When the SMS is received, the **autofill code bar** appears above the keyboard.
2. At the same time, the Xcode console prints layout constraint warnings like:

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: const Text("Test"),
        ),
        body: Column(
          children: [
            Expanded(
                child: Container(
              color: Colors.blue,
            )),
            Expanded(
                child: TextField(
              minLines: null,
              maxLines: null,
              expands: true,
              textAlignVertical: TextAlignVertical.top,
              keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
              controller: MyTextEditingController(),
              //specialTextSpanBuilder: MySpecialTextSpanBuilder(),
            ))
          ],
        ), // This trailing comma makes auto-formatting nicer for build methods.
      ),
    );
  }
}

class MyTextEditingController extends TextEditingController {
  @override
  TextSpan buildTextSpan(
      {required BuildContext context,
      TextStyle? style,
      required bool withComposing}) {
    return TextSpan(
      children: [
        // const TextSpan(text: '1'), // Adding this textspan fixes the issue
        WidgetSpan(
          child: Container(
            height: 1000,
            color: Colors.red,
          ),
        )
      ],
    );
  }
}
```
